info.name: Giftyzel Plugin API
info.description:
**Giftyzel Plugin API – Full integration guide**

**1. Where to get your plugin API key**
- Vendor dashboard → Store setup → Plugin API key → Create key.
- Or ask Giftyzel admin to create one for your store.
- The key looks like `gfty_live_xxx` (or `gfty_test_xxx` in test).

**2. How to configure this Postman collection**
- Set `base_url` to your backend base (production: `https://www.giftyzel.com`, use sandbox base if provided).
- Set `api_key` to your full plugin API key value.
- All requests in this collection already read `{{api_key}}` and send it in the `X-API-Key` header.

**3. Authentication options (choose one per request)**
- **Recommended:** header `X-API-Key: {{api_key}}` (already pre-filled in each request).
- **Alternative:** header `Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}`.
- Do **not** use your login JWT here – only the plugin key works.

**4. Typical flow for plugin checkout**
1) Call **Validate API key** once when you configure the plugin, to confirm the key is valid.
2) When a customer is ready to pay on your store, call **Create payment + redirect to Stripe (hosted page)** (this is the only supported flow):
   - Send sender details, `receiver_email`, and `items`.
   - Giftyzel creates a Stripe Checkout Session with all data in the underlying PaymentIntent `metadata` and returns a `url`.
   - Your frontend redirects the customer to `url` (Stripe hosted page) to enter card details and pay.
3) Stripe charges the customer and calls the Giftyzel webhook after `payment_intent.succeeded`. The webhook creates an order in Giftyzel automatically – no extra order-creation API is needed.
4) Use **List orders** or **Get order by Giftyzel order id** to sync order data into your system.
5) Use **Update order status (PATCH)** when your store dispatches or delivers the order so Giftyzel stays in sync.

**5. Test environment tips**
- Use Stripe test card `4242 4242 4242 4242` with any future expiry and CVC.
- You can test the full redirect flow using the internal page `/plugin-checkout` in the Giftyzel app, or by calling the redirect request in this collection and opening the returned `url` in the browser.
- In test, Giftyzel still creates real orders in its database; just mark them as tests in your own system.

variable.description: Full plugin API key (starts with gfty_live_…). Use in X-API-Key header or as Authorization: Bearer {{api_key}}. Get it from Admin.

item.name: Create payment + redirect to Stripe (hosted page)

request.description:
Create a Stripe Checkout Session and redirect the buyer to Stripe's **hosted Checkout page** (recommended and only supported flow).

**How it works**
- Send sender details, `receiver_email`, and `items[]`.
- Include `redirect_checkout: true` in the body.
- Optionally override `success_url` and `cancel_url` (otherwise Giftyzel defaults are used and point to the order status page).
- The response contains a `url` to Stripe Checkout and `session_id`.
- Redirect the customer to `url`; Stripe shows the amount and card form, collects the payment, and redirects back to `success_url` or `cancel_url`.
- After payment succeeds, the Giftyzel webhook creates the order automatically.

Quick health check for your plugin API key.

- Sends `X-API-Key: {{api_key}}`.
- `200` response = key is valid and linked to an active plugin integration.
- `401` response = missing/invalid key.

Use this when you first configure the plugin (or when rotating keys) so you can fail fast if the key is wrong.

Paginated list of all plugin orders for **your** integration.

- Uses your API key to scope results to your store only.
- Optional query params: `page` (default 1), `limit` (default 20, max 50).
- Response shape: `{ orders: [...], pagination: { page, limit, total } }`.

Use this for admin list views (e.g. in your plugin backend UI) or for periodic sync jobs.

item.name: Get order by Giftyzel order id

request.description:
Fetch a **single** order by its primary Giftyzel id (`orders.id` UUID).

- This is the recommended way to load full order details after it has been created by the webhook.
- Use the `id` returned in webhooks / admin dashboards / list responses.

Typical usage: your system stores the Giftyzel `order_id` and later calls this endpoint to show the latest status and receiver details.

Optional convenience lookup by your own **external_order_id**.

- Only works if you passed `external_order_id` when you initiated the payment.
- Useful when your system has its own order id and you just want to attach the Giftyzel/Stripe data later.

Update the lifecycle status of an order **after** it has been created.

- Allowed values: `pending`, `confirmed`, `dispatched`, `delivered`, `cancelled`.
- Example body: `{ "status": "dispatched" }`.

Use this from your backend when the store confirms, ships or delivers the gift so that Giftyzel and the admin dashboard always show the correct status.
